findings.location: s7 (複雑なパッケージへのディスパッチ)
findings.description: Source contains a concrete example table showing how request paths map to dispatch target classes (e.g., `/admin/AdminApp` → `nablarch.sample.apps1.admin.AdminApp`, `/user/UserApp` → `nablarch.sample.apps2.user.UserApp`, `/BaseApp` → `nablarch.sample.base.BaseApp`). This table is omitted from the knowledge file. The table is the only place that demonstrates how the `//` suffix in `requestPattern` (e.g., `/admin//`) results in sub-path components being appended to the basePackage to form the fully-qualified class name.
